task: Write a Python function trimmed_mean(nums) returning the mean of the list after dropping exactly one occurrence of the minimum and one occurrence of the maximum, as a float. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function correctly calculates the trimmed mean, but could be improved with additional comments for clarity.
trace: To implement the trimmed mean function, I first checked if the input list is empty or has fewer than three elements, and in such cases returned None to adhere to the stated edge case. Then, I sorted the list and calculated the indices of the minimum and maximum values while making sure not to go out of bounds when the list is too small. After that, I summed up the middle elements (excluding the minimum and maximum) and divided by the number of middle elements (plus one for the initial min_idx) to calculate the trimmed mean.
